Off Calendar Jan-26-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

PLAINTIFF EXPONENT, INCORPORATED'S Motion For Leave To File Its Second Amended Complaint IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-25-2009 Set For Trial On Mar-15-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Feb-26-10 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 503

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Evidentiary Sanctions; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf (Ccp 2023.030, 2030.300, 2031.310)
Off Calendar Aug-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
JUAN C. ARANDA, Atty. for defendant and moving party: KENNETH BAKER, HILL INTERNATIONAL
BRENT F. BASILICO, Atty. for Plaintiff and Opposing party: EXPONENT, INC.

RULING: HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendants Kenneth Baker, Hill International, Inc.'s Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative, Evidentiary Sanctions; Request For Monetary Sanctions is denied in part and granted in part. Defendants' requests for terminating, issue and/or evidentiary sanctions are denied, without prejudice. Defendants' request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $2,000, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's repeated failures to respond to discovery requests and to this Court's discovery orders were an abuse of the discovery process. Hearing required. Plaintiff is to report at the hearing whether it has paid the $590 in sanctions ordered by the Court on June 17, 2009. Order was signed in open court. JUDGE RICHARD B. ULMER, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-17-2009 Continued To Sep-04-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Juan Araneda, Esq. of Long & Levit, LLP, for defendant and moving party Hill International Inc & Kenneth Baker
James Ficenec, Esq. of Sellar Hazard Manning Ficenec & Lai, 925-938-1430 for plaintiff and opposing party Exponent, Inc.

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANTS KENNETH BAKER, HILL INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. EVEN ASSUMING THE ILLEGALITY OF THE NON SOLICITATION PROVISION, THE COURT WOULD NOT DETERMINE AS A MATTER OF LAW ON DEMURRER THAT INDEPENDENT PROVISIONS OF THE CONTRACT VIOLATED BY DIFFERENT CONDUCT COULD NOT SUPPORT A BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance With Discovery Req.'S And Req. For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Juan C. Araneda, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Kenneth R. Baker
No other appearances.

THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: Defendants Kenneth Baker, Hill International, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Compliance With Discovery Requests is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $590, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-18-2009 Continued To Jul-24-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Juan Araneda of Long & Levit (397.2222) for defendants and moving parties
William Manning of Sellar Hazard Manning Ficenec & Lai (925.938.1430) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS KENNETH BAKER, AND HILL INTERNATIONAL, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled as to all causes of action other than breach of contract (1st cause of action), which is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead interpretation of the clause that is not barred by state statute and policy, if plaintiff can do so in good faith. The allegations sufficiently allege information that could be protected as trade secrets. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. (301/PJB/xLT) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189
